channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Kuwait frees a group of jailed Americans, including contractors held on drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kuwait has released a group of American prisoners, including veterans and military contractors jailed for years on drug-related charges, in a move seen as a gesture of goodwill between two allies, a representative for the detainees told The Associated Press on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada cuts wait time for inmates needing mental health care
item.description: The wait time for inmates needing a mental health bed to regain competency was down to an average 74 days compared to 78 when the 8 News Now Investigators reported on the statewide problem last fall.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge hopes to rule next week on Trump order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A federal judge says she hopes to rule next week on whether to block President Donald Trump’s administration from banning transgender people from serving in the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New York assemblyman tries to confront Tom Homan over arrest of Columbia University anti-Israel activist
item.description: New York state Assembly member and New York City mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani tried to confront border czar Tom Homan during his visit to Albany on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Windsor Park deadlines loom, construction yet to start decades after homes began to sink
item.description: About $37 million is allocated to build 93 new homes for people in Windsor Park, a North Las Vegas neighborhood that began sinking decades ago. Now as deadlines are looming, lawmakers learn that construction has yet to begin.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota House is tied after DFL wins special election. Here’s what changes
item.description: DFLer David Gottfried won the suburban Ramsey County seat and is expected to be sworn in to the House of Representatives next week.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: A Republican-backed bill would upend voter registration. Here are 8 things to know
item.description: Congressional Republicans are pushing legislation that would make sweeping changes to voter registration, including requiring those signing up to present documents proving U.S. citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: The European Union retaliates after the U.S. metals tariffs take hold
item.description: The European Union on Wednesday announced retaliatory trade action with a series of duties on U.S. products, responding to the Trump administration rise in tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate Democrats appear ready to back down in government shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Democrats say the six-month government funding resolution that passed the House Tuesday is a “horrible” bill, but there’s growing sentiment within the Senate Democratic conference that it would be too risky to block the legislation and risk a government shutdown that could drag on for weeks.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump vows to take back 'stolen' wealth as tariffs on steel and aluminum imports go into effect
item.description: President Donald Trump is openly challenging U.S. allies by increasing tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports to 25% as he vows to take back wealth he says was “stolen” by other countries, drawing quick retaliation from Europe and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Paul Wiseman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House GOP passes spending bill, sending plan to avert government shutdown to Senate
item.description: The bill puts significant pressure on Democrats in the Senate, whose votes will be needed in order to avoid a shutdown. The federal government runs out of money at the end of the day on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh and Claudia Grisales, MPR News

item.title: As Trump downplays economic fears, this data shows people feel differently
item.description: Economists look for signs that a recession may be approaching by monitoring consumer confidence and business sentiment — two indicators of uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: The latest: Trump says he'll double planned tariffs on Canadian aluminum and steel
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump says he’ll double planned tariffs on Canadian aluminum and steel, sticking with a trade war despite Monday’s stock market drop. That and more of the latest with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump's tariffs raise concerns of economic recession
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s tariffs on products from Canada and Mexico have some Americans worried that they will lead the economy into a recession.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Mayor Adams’ approval rating reaches record low ahead of election
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – The mayor’s race is heating up just as Mayor Eric Adams’ approval rating hits a new low, according to a new poll from Quinnipiac University. A new poll shows only 20% of voters approve of how Adams is handling his post, while 67% disapprove.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs on Canadian dairy, lumber products as soon as Friday
item.description: Access to dairy and lumber markets have long been two of the biggest points of contention in the otherwise strong economic relationship between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security offices in NY set to close amid DOGE budget cuts
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Several Social Security offices in New York will be closing as the Department of Government Efficiency continues to cut federal spending. The closures come after the Social Security Administration said it found over $800 million in savings for the 2025 fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Aces could join Las Vegas teams with their own specialty license plates
item.description: Las Vegas Aces fans might get a chance to show their support as the state considers issuing a specialty license plate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tampa Bay Ukrainian refugees worried temporary legal status could be revoked
item.description: It has been three years since Russia invaded Ukraine sparking the latest deadly conflict between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
